channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: President Marcos stresses improving infrastructure to enhance tourism in the Philippines
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. emphasized the need to improve the Philippines' infrastructure to strengthen the country's tourism industry. Speaking during the inauguration of the Mactan Ex...

item.title: Oil prices decline amid US-Iran tensions over Strait of Hormuz security
item.description: Oil prices fell on Monday after early gains, following U.S. President Donald Trump's call for other nations to assist in safeguarding the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for global oil and gas ship...

item.title: High-Demand Biology Careers and Expected Salaries in Kenya
item.description: For those keen on biology, there are several career paths with strong demand and good pay in Kenya. Graduates can explore roles like medical doctors, pharmacologists, forensic scientists, biotechnolog...

item.title: Tanzania’s Digital Growth: Opportunities and Cyber Risks
item.description: Tanzania is going through big changes with technology, and digital tools are now changing how people live, work, and trade. Farmers can check market prices online before selling, small business owners...

item.title: MP Primary School Teacher Suspended for Imitating PM Modi in Classroom Over LPG Price Discussion
item.description: In Madhya Pradesh, a government primary school teacher has been suspended following an incident in which he mimicked Prime Minister Narendra Modi while discussing LPG prices. The act reportedly took p...

item.title: Bank lending changes to drive rise in apartment completions across Australia
item.description: Australia is set to experience a significant rise in new apartment completions this year as major banks relax lending conditions for property developers. According to new data from consultancy firm Ch...

item.title: Steven Dubois defends 500m title at World Short Track Championships
item.description: Steven Dubois of Terrebonne, Quebec, successfully defended his world title in the men's 500-metre short track speedskating event at the ISU World Championships in Montreal on March 14, 2026. Despite b...

item.title: Ministry of Foreign Affairs inquiries Rs4 million cash kept by low-paid staff in Gujrat
item.description: GUJRAT – The Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MoFA) has initiated an official inquiry after Rs4 million in cash was reportedly found at the residence of two daily-wage officials, Amir Shaukat and Irfan Ah...

item.title: Government Announces Youth Jobs Grant to Boost Young Employment
item.description: The government has unveiled a new initiative, the Youth Jobs Grant, aimed at tackling youth unemployment in the UK. The plan involves providing grants to businesses that hire young people aged 18-24 w...

item.title: Dennis Walder Explores His Mother's Past in a Memoir of Family Secrets and Memory
item.description: Dennis Walder, a South African-born literary scholar, has recently released a memoir titled 'Amid the Alien Corn: A Son's Memoir', in which he reflects on his lifelong quest to understand his mother R...

item.title: Counties to Spearhead Efforts Against Food Insecurity in Kenya
item.description: Kenya faces serious food insecurity, especially in rural areas where many families struggle to get enough food due to poor harvests and climate challenges. The article argues that county governments s...

item.description: The phrase 'no quarter' has become a point of concern following statements by United States Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth, who said the U.S. would continue military operations against Iran with 'no q...

item.title: Flooded Roads and Burglaries Disrupt Limpopo Schools
item.description: The Limpopo Department of Education has temporarily suspended schooling for two days in several districts hit hard by continuous heavy rains and the risk of flash floods. Vhembe and Mopani districts r...

item.title: Senegal Faces Critical Debt Challenges Amid Calls for Restructuring
item.description: Senegal is going through a serious debt problem, wit debt at 132% of GDP as of end 2024, and paying debt is consuming a bigger chunk of government revenue. Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko say no to debt ...

item.title: Guzman y Gomez co-CEO reduces workload as he prepares for kidney transplant
item.description: Hilton Brett, co-chief executive of Guzman y Gomez, will be scaling back his day-to-day responsibilities as he prepares for a kidney transplant later this year. The company has assured investors that ...

item.title: Dolly Parton Discusses Her Health and Grieving Process at Dollywood’s 41st Season Opening
item.description: Country music legend Dolly Parton made a heartfelt public return on March 13, 2026, at the grand opening of Dollywood’s 41st season. Speaking to an enthusiastic crowd, the 80-year-old artist reflected...
